message:
Refactor Telegram client with unified request handler and retry logic

Consolidates HTTP request handling into a single generic request<T, B>()
method with comprehensive retry and error handling:

Retry logic:
- Retries up to 3 times on:
  - HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) - respects Retry-After header
  - Server errors (5xx)
  - Connection/timeout errors
- Exponential backoff starting at 100ms, doubling each attempt
- Logs each retry attempt with context

Error handling:
- Parses Telegram API error codes and descriptions
- Distinguishes between HTTP transport errors and logical API errors
- Checks TelegramError::is_retryable() for logical error retry decisions

Refactored methods:
- get_me(): Uses request("GET", "getMe", ...)
- get_updates(): Uses request("POST", "getUpdates", ...) with custom timeout
- send_message(): Uses request() with parse mode fallback on error
- answer_callback_query(): Converted from form params to JSON body

get_file() retains manual implementation due to query parameter requirements
that don't fit the JSON-body pattern.

This improves reliability when Telegram API has transient issues and
reduces code duplication across the client methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
